title: Quantum Neural Network 3D — A Deep Dive into Interactive WebGL Visualization
summary: A developer created Quantum Neural Network 3D, an interactive 3D visualization of neural networks using Three.js and WebGL with multiple formation types and real-time user interactions. The project demonstrates advanced web graphics programming combining mathematical modeling, particle effects, and smooth animations to create an immersive experience.
content:
Introduction


In the modern era of web development, static interfaces are no longer enough. Users expect immersive, interactive, and visually engaging experiences. With this vision in mind, I developed Quantum Neural Network 3D — an advanced real-time 3D neural network visualization built using Three.js and WebGL.
This project is not just about visuals; it represents a combination of mathematics, graphics programming and user interaction design, creating a simulation that feels alive and responsive.
Quantum Neural Network 3D is an interactive system that visually represents neural structures in a three-dimensional space. It allows users to explore different network formations, trigger energy pulses and interact with a dynamically animated environment.
The goal of this project was to push beyond basic frontend development and explore advanced rendering techniques, shader programming and real-time interactivity.
The core of the project is a fully dynamic neural network composed of nodes and connections. Each node behaves like an energy point and connections simulate data flow between them.
The system supports different structural patterns:
Crystalline Sphere — based on mathematical distribution (golden ratio)
Helix Lattice — inspired by DNA-like structures
Fractal Web — recursive branching similar to AI networks
Each formation provides a unique visual and structural experience.
Breathing node effects
Flowing connection lines
Smooth transitions between formations
Continuous rotation and motion
These animations create a lifelike and futuristic feel.
Users can click anywhere on the screen to generate energy pulses, which travel across the network in real-time. This feature adds a layer of interaction and makes the system feel responsive and intelligent.
Bloom (glow) effects
Particle-based starfield background
Smooth camera controls
Glass morphism UI design
These elements combine to deliver a premium visual experience.
The project is designed to be fully interactive:
Cli

zh.title: 量子神经网络3D — 交互式WebGL可视化深度探讨
zh.summary: 开发者创建了量子神经网络3D，这是一个使用Three.js和WebGL的神经网络交互式3D可视化，包含多种编队类型和实时用户交互。该项目展示了先进的网络图形编程，结合了数学建模、粒子效应和流畅动画以创建沉浸式体验。

title: How To Install Caveman In Codex On macOS And Windows
summary: Caveman is a local plugin for Codex in VS Code that compresses AI responses to be more concise and technical while preserving meaning. This guide provides step-by-step setup instructions for both macOS and Windows using local plugin folders and marketplace configuration.
content:
If you use Codex inside VS Code and want shorter, more compressed responses, caveman is a simple local plugin that adds that behavior.
This guide shows the full setup for both macOS and Windows. It uses a local plugin plus a local marketplace entry, so you do not need to publish anything remotely.
caveman adds a skill that tells Codex to answer in a much tighter style:
fewer filler words
shorter phrasing
preserved technical meaning
optional stronger compression levels
It can also auto-activate on session start if you add the optional .codex hook files.
The plugin is made of three parts:
a plugin folder: plugins/caveman

a marketplace file: .agents/plugins/marketplace.json

optional Codex hook files: .codex/hooks.json and .codex/config.toml




  
  
  Folder Layout


Your workspace should end up looking like this:
your-project/
├─ .agents/
│  └─ plugins/
│     └─ marketplace.json
├─ .codex/
│  ├─ config.toml
│  └─ hooks.json
└─ plugins/
   └─ caveman/
      ├─ .codex-plugin/
      │  └─ plugin.json
      ├─ assets/
      │  ├─ caveman-small.svg
      │  └─ caveman.svg
      └─ skills/
         └─ caveman/
            └─ SKILL.md

Use this if you want caveman available only in one repo or one VS Code workspace.
If your project lives at:
/Users/your-name/code/my-app

create these folders inside that project:
/Users/your-name/code/my-app/plugins/caveman
/Users/your-name/code/my-app/.agents/plugins
/Users/your-name/code/my-app/.codex

If your project lives at:
C:\Users\your-name\code\my-app

create these folders inside that project:
C:\Users\your-name\code\my-app\plugins\caveman
C:\Users\your-name\code\my-app\.agents\plugins
C:\Users\your-name\code\my-app\.codex

Use this if you want caveman available across multiple projects.
~/plugins/caveman
~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json

If you also want Codex hooks globally, use:
~/.codex/hooks.json
~/.codex/config.toml

C:\Users\your-name\plugins\caveman
C:\Users\your-name\.agents\plugins\marketplace.json

If you also want Co

zh.title: 如何在macOS和Windows上在Codex中安装Caveman
zh.summary: Caveman是VS Code中Codex的本地插件，可以压缩AI响应使其更简洁和技术性，同时保留意义。本指南为macOS和Windows提供了分步骤设置说明，使用本地插件文件夹和市场配置。

title: Automation Pipeline Reliability: Why Your Workflow Breaks When Nobody Is Watching
summary: Most automation pipelines fail silently because teams focus on individual step success rather than end-to-end completion, leading to stale data and missed deadlines that users only discover after problems compound. Proper automation reliability requires monitoring full pipeline completion, not just confirming individual jobs started or exited successfully.
content:
If your deployment, sync, backup, or report workflow depends on scheduled or chained automation, automation pipeline reliability is not a nice-to-have. It is the difference between a smooth system and a production mess that quietly grows for hours.
A lot of teams assume their pipeline is reliable because each step works most of the time. The build passes, the cron job exists, the queue worker is running, the logs look fine. But in the real world, automation pipelines fail in ways that are annoyingly silent. A job starts late, a webhook never arrives, a worker hangs halfway through, or one skipped task breaks everything downstream.
The painful part is that nobody notices until users complain, data goes stale, or a release window is already gone.
Most automation pipelines are made of multiple small moving parts:
a scheduler or cron trigger
a CI/CD workflow
one or more scripts or workers
external APIs
retries and queues
notifications or downstream updates
On paper, that sounds robust. In practice, every extra step adds another place where execution can stop without a clear signal.
Imagine a simple workflow like this:
A cron job triggers every hour
It exports fresh billing data
Another task transforms the file
A worker uploads it to a partner API
A final step sends a Slack or email summary
If step 1 never fires, nothing happens.
This is why automation pipeline reliability is often misunderstood. People focus on whether a task can run, not whether the whole chain actually completed on time.
Automation pipelines become unreliable for a few recurring reasons.
A pipeline often relies on system cron, environment variables, network connectivity, queue health, database access, file permissions, and external services. If any one of those fails, the rest of the pipeline may never run.
The pipeline is only as reliable as its weakest dependency.
Many teams treat “job started” or “process exited with code 0” as proof that the automation worked. That is a weak signal.
A script can e

zh.title: 自动化管道可靠性：为什么您的工作流在没有人监督时会崩溃
zh.summary: 大多数自动化管道以静默方式失败，因为团队专注于单个步骤的成功而不是端到端的完成，导致数据陈旧和用户只在问题复合后才发现的错过截止日期。适当的自动化可靠性需要监控完整的管道完成，而不仅仅是确认单个工作已启动或成功退出。

title: I Built an 'Open World' AI Coding Agent — It Works From ANY Folder
summary: A developer created a persistent memory system for OpenCode that maintains context across different folders and sessions, transforming it from a stateless tool into a persistent collaborator with global memory, custom skills, and auto-recovery. The system survives folder changes and provides git-based management for safe upgrades.
content:
The Problem


Every AI coding agent I've used has the same fatal flaw: it forgets when you close the session.
Open from folder A → works great
Forgetting context isn't just annoying. It's a productivity killer.
I built a memory system for OpenCode that transforms it from a stateless assistant into a persistent collaborator.
~/.config/opencode/
├── .global/              ← YOUR WORKSPACE
│   ├── skills/          ← Custom skills
│   ├── memory/           ← Persistent memory
│   └── projects/         ← Work tracking
├── skills/              ← Base skills
└── plugins/             ← System plugins

The magic: .global/ folder survives folder changes.
Memory persists across ALL folders. Your preferences, decisions, and context follow you everywhere.
Want to test a new skill? Create it in .global/skills/. It runs without touching the base installation. Broken? Delete it. Base unaffected.
Crash recovery built-in. The agent auto-restores from backup if something breaks.
git clone https://github.com/Kshitiz-Maurya/opencode-enhanced.git
.\install.ps1
# Done!




Feature
Traditional AI Agent
My Creation




Memory
Session only
Global (all folders)


Upgrades
Risk breaking
Sandbox safe


Setup
Manual
One-command


Recovery
Manual
Auto-healing


Sharing
Copy files
Git clone



# Clone the starter kit
git clone https://github.com/Kshitiz-Maurya/opencode-enhanced.git

# Run install
.\install.ps1

# Restart OpenCode
# Done! Memory persists from any folder

This is v1. I'm testing it myself before public launch. But the concept is solid: an AI that remembers, learns, and grows with you — not just in one project, but across your entire development workflow.
What do you think? Would a persistent-memory AI coding agent help your workflow?
Built with OpenCode. The architecture is MIT licensed — fork it and make it yours.

zh.title: 我构建了一个"开放世界" AI 编码代理 — 它可以在任何文件夹中工作
zh.summary: 一位开发者为 OpenCode 创建了一个持久化记忆系统，可以在不同的文件夹和会话中保持上下文，将其从无状态工具转变为具有全局记忆、自定义技能和自动恢复能力的持久合作者。该系统可以在文件夹变更时保存，并提供基于 git 的管理来确保安全升级。

title: From Freelancing to Product: A Tech Service Company's SaaS Transformation
summary: Ultra Lab transitioned from pure freelancing to a hybrid model by productizing repetitive client deliverables into SaaS subscriptions for social media automation, video production, and website services. The company maintains both revenue streams, currently at 60% freelancing and 40% SaaS subscriptions, with plans to flip the ratio.
content:
The Freelancing Ceiling


Freelancing (contract development / outsourced dev work) is the fastest way for a technical person to start earning money. You have the skills, clients have the needs -- money for deliverables, simple as that.
But freelancing has a fatal flaw: your income = your time x your hourly rate.
No matter how good you are, there are only 24 hours in a day. When your schedule is fully booked, your income hits a ceiling. Want to earn more? Either raise your rate or work overtime -- both have hard limits.
That's why we decided to shift from freelancing to productization.
Ultra Lab started as a pure freelancing operation. Clients came to us to build websites, systems, and AI integrations. We quoted, developed, and delivered.
Revenue breakdown: 100% freelance income.
After completing a dozen-plus projects, we noticed a pattern: many clients wanted similar things.
3 clients wanted a "social media auto-posting system"
2 clients wanted "automated short-form video production"
5 clients wanted a "brand website + forms + notifications"
Building from scratch every time? That's wasteful.
We decided to productize our most repetitive deliverables into SaaS subscription services:



Original Freelance Service
After Productization
Pricing




Building Threads automation for clients
Multi-Account Threads Automation System
NT$1,990/mo


Building IG Reel publishing systems for clients
Fully Automated IG Reel Publishing System
NT$2,999/mo


Creating short-form videos for clients
Automated Short-Form Video Production System
NT$999/mo



We kept our custom freelance services (SaaS development, AI integration, tech consulting) for clients with unique requirements.
Current revenue breakdown: freelancing 60% + SaaS subscriptions 40% (the goal is to flip that ratio).
Many people want to jump straight into SaaS and skip the freelancing phase. That's risky.
The value of freelancing isn't just the money -- it's market research. Through freelancing, you learn:
What clients are ac

zh.title: 从自由职业到产品：一家科技服务公司的 SaaS 转型
zh.summary: Ultra Lab 通过将重复性的客户交付物转变为 SaaS 订阅服务（包括社交媒体自动化、视频制作和网站服务），从纯自由职业过渡到混合模式。该公司保持两种收入流，目前处于 60% 自由职业和 40% SaaS 订阅的比例，计划翻转这个比例。

title: China's AI Giants: Adding Tencent Hunyuan & ByteDance Doubao to AI University (74 Providers)
summary: The developer added Tencent Hunyuan and ByteDance Doubao to their AI University platform, bringing the total provider count to 74, with Tencent offering competitive multimodal open-source models across text, image, video, and 3D generation. ByteDance's Doubao 2.0 emphasizes agent-focused capabilities with pricing reportedly 3.7x cheaper than GPT-5.2.
content:
China's AI Giants: Tencent Hunyuan & ByteDance Doubao — AI University Update


I've added Tencent Hunyuan and ByteDance Doubao to the AI University in my app, bringing the total provider count to 74 companies. With Baidu already registered, China's "BAT" trio is now complete.
Tencent's AI brand stands out by releasing world-class open-source models across ALL modalities: text, image, video, and 3D.



Model
Scale

389B MoE (52B active)
Largest OSS MoE at release / 256K context

80B
World's largest OSS image generation model

13B+

—
Single image → 3D mesh

Most AI companies specialize in one modality. Tencent offers competitive open-source models in text, image, video, AND 3D from a single organization — backed by production experience from WeChat and QQ.
# Tencent Cloud SDK
from tencentcloud.hunyuan.v20230901 import hunyuan_client, models

req = models.ChatCompletionsRequest()
req.Model = "hunyuan-turbo"  # Fast + cost-effective
req.Messages = [{"Role": "user", "Content": "Write a Deno Edge Function"}]

Model

Code-specialized


Seedance 2.0
TikTok-integrated video generation



Doubao API is reportedly 3.7x cheaper than GPT-5.2 — significant for high-volume batch processing.

  method: 'POST',
  headers: {
    'Authorization': `Bearer ${Deno.env.get('DOUBAO_API_KEY')}`,
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    model: 'doubao-seed-1-6-250615',

zh.title: 中国 AI 巨头：向 AI University 添加腾讯混元和字节跳动豆包（74 个供应商）
zh.summary: 开发者向其 AI University 平台添加了腾讯混元和字节跳动豆包，使供应商总数达到 74 个，其中腾讯提供具有竞争力的多模态开源模型，覆盖文本、图像、视频和 3D 生成。字节跳动的豆包 2.0 强调以 agent 为中心的能力，其价格据报道比 GPT-5.2 便宜 3.7 倍。

title: On the Vibe Coders and Their Lies
summary: The author reflects on building Hako, a 5,000+ line C-based text editor developed over several years, incorporating AI assistance to scaffold sections and brainstorm new features during development. The piece examines the reality of "vibe coding" and AI-assisted development beyond the hype.
content:
Let me tell you what I was doing while Andrej Karpathy was coining the term "vibe coding."
I was writing a single-file, modal text editor in C. For years, my primary editor was vim; not neovim, yes, regular old vim. Not an IDE person, but my intention in writing this isn't to make you a terminal supremacist. Some time back, I found snaptoken's "Build Your Own Text Editor" tutorial, based on antirez's original kilo, and kind of just spun out from there. I finished the tutorial, though I still had an itch to scratch and wanted to dive further into both the C language and the editors themselves: just me, a terminal, and a lot of segfaults. Before long, it was generally usable, and I was constantly reaching for Vim motions that weren't there. I started building these out, then I wanted split paneling, built that out. Then I wanted another feature, and another, ultimately growing beyond the time I'd set aside to work on it. Life and family are busy. By the time I was done, Hako (Japanese for "box"), the editor, was north of 5,000 lines, and yet I still had ideas. I was still missing vim functions I needed, and I had begun thinking about integrating AI into both the workflow and the project itself.

The idea was to create something like what Codex or Claude Code eventually became, though living inside an editor I had built, and what's more, the ability to use any model you want with it, local or otherwise. I have actually been working on a Vim plugin with much the same ability. Hako will have this soon. Now, I am not glossing over the "into the workflow" part of what. I started having Claude scaffold out sections that I planned to work on, brainstorm new paths that diverge from code I already wrote, and so on. Before I knew it, I had something functional, useful, and that I reached for daily. I used Hako to write code through a large portion of my computer science degree, including the courses on the very systems concepts it was built on.
This isn't a flex. There is a poi

zh.title: 关于Vibe编程者和他们的谎言
zh.summary: 作者反思了Hako的开发过程，这是一个5000多行的C语言文本编辑器，花费数年开发，在开发中融入了AI辅助来生成代码段和集思广益新功能。该文章审视了"氛围编程"和AI辅助开发的真实情况，超越了市场炒作。

title: clerk: Auto-Summarize Your Claude Code Sessions
summary: Clerk automatically summarizes Claude Code sessions when they end and organizes summaries by date and project, eliminating the need for manual context preservation. The tool also enables semantic search across past work and one-command session resumption without remembering session IDs.
content:
The Friday Afternoon Problem


It's 4 PM on Friday. Your manager asks for the weekly report. You stare at your terminal, trying to remember what you did on Monday. Was it the auth refactor or the API migration? Which project was that even in?
You open git log. Scroll. Scroll. Scroll. Piece together commit messages across 4 repositories. Thirty minutes later, you have a rough draft that still feels incomplete.
Sound familiar?
But it's not just Fridays. Every morning, you open Claude Code and realize you forgot to use --resume. Yesterday's session had all the context — the architecture decisions, the debugging steps, the half-finished plan. Now it's gone, buried in a pile of session IDs.
That's what clerk does. Install once, forget about it. Every session is automatically summarized when it ends.
brew install vulcanshen/tap/clerk
clerk install

No configuration needed. No commands to remember. No habits to build.
clerk runs entirely on your machine — no remote services, no accounts, no data leaving your laptop. All you need is Claude Code.
Every time a Claude Code session ends, clerk processes the transcript and saves a structured summary as a markdown file, organized by date and project:
~/.clerk/
├── summary/
│   ├── 20260414/
│   │   ├── projects-my-api.md
│   │   └── work-frontend.md
│   └── 20260416/
│       ├── projects-my-api.md
│       └── work-frontend.md
├── tags/
│   ├── auth.md
│   ├── vue.md
│   └── refactor.md
└── ...

Forgot --resume? No problem. Just type /clerk-resume in Claude Code:
Claude reads your past summaries and transcripts, rebuilds the context, and you're back where you left off.
Need to find that session where you worked on authentication?
Type /clerk-search and say "auth". clerk uses AI-powered semantic matching — it doesn't just string-match tags, it understands that "auth" relates to "jwt", "oauth", "login".
This is the one that saves Friday afternoons:
clerk report --days 7

clerk reads all summaries from the past 7 days, sends them to

zh.title: clerk: 自动总结Claude Code会话
zh.summary: Clerk在Claude Code会话结束时自动总结它们，并按日期和项目组织这些总结，消除了手动保存上下文的必要。该工具还支持对过去工作进行语义搜索，以及无需记住会话ID就能一键恢复会话。

title: Why SNMP Monitoring Misses 80% of Network Problems — And What to Use Instead
summary: SNMP monitoring only captures device-level metrics and misses application-layer problems like TCP retransmission rates, DNS delays, and TLS failures that directly impact users but never appear in standard counters. Real network visibility requires packet-level inspection to catch these silent failures before users report them.
content:
If your network monitoring strategy relies primarily on SNMP polling, you're flying blind to most of the problems that actually cause downtime, slowdowns, and user complaints.
That's not an exaggeration. Here's why.
SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol) polls devices — routers, switches, firewalls — for counters: interface utilization, CPU load, memory usage, error counts, uplink status.
It answers questions like:
Is this link up or down?
What's the bandwidth utilization on port Gi0/1?
How many CRC errors did this interface accumulate?
For capacity planning and device health, SNMP is fine. It's been fine for 30 years.
Here's the problem: most real-world network issues don't show up in SNMP counters.
A user reports "the app is slow." You check SNMP — all links are up, utilization is under 40%, no errors. Everything looks green.
But the actual problem is a 3% TCP retransmission rate between the application server and the database. That 3% adds 200-400ms of latency to every transaction. SNMP will never show you this because it doesn't look at packet-level behavior.
A misconfigured or overloaded DNS server adds 2-3 seconds to every new connection. Users experience random slowdowns. SNMP shows the DNS server is "up" with low CPU usage.
The only way to see this is to inspect the actual DNS query/response pairs and measure resolution time — packet by packet.
A certificate expires, or a client and server can't agree on a cipher suite. Connections fail silently. SNMP counters might show a slight uptick in TCP resets, but won't tell you why.
Full packet capture shows you the exact TLS ClientHello, the ServerHello (or lack thereof), and the precise failure point.
SMB file transfers timing out. HTTP 502 errors from a reverse proxy. SIP call quality degradation. Database query timeouts.
None of these show up in SNMP. They live in the packet payload — in the application-layer protocol behavior that SNMP was never designed to inspect.
The worst kind of network problem: it happ

zh.title: 为什么SNMP监控遗漏80%的网络问题——以及如何替代
zh.summary: SNMP监控只捕获设备级指标，遗漏应用层问题，如TCP重传率、DNS延迟和TLS失败，这些问题直接影响用户但永远不会出现在标准计数器中。真正的网络可见性需要数据包级检查来在用户报告之前捕获这些无声的故障。

title: CrewAI vs LangGraph vs AutoGen: Which Framework for Production AI Agents?
summary: Drawing from 18 months of production deployments, this comparison examines three AI agent frameworks—CrewAI (crew metaphor), LangGraph (state graph), and AutoGen (message-passing actors)—explaining their mental models and real-world trade-offs beyond toy benchmarks. The choice between them depends on use case structure, team expertise, and operational maturity.
content:
We've shipped production agents on all three frameworks in the last 18 months. Here's the honest comparison most tutorials won't give you.
Every other week a new agent framework trends on GitHub, and every other week a tech lead asks us the same question: "Which one should we actually build on?" The short answer is: it depends on what you're building, who's building it, and how mature your ops practice is. The long answer is this article.
This is not a benchmark post. Benchmarks on toy tasks tell you almost nothing about how a framework behaves when a retrieval call times out at 2 a.m., a tool returns malformed JSON, or the product team asks for a human approval step on step 7 of a 12-step workflow. What follows is a field report from real deployments, including the parts that hurt.
CrewAI models agents as roles on a crew. You define agents (researcher, writer, critic), give them goals and backstories, and compose them into sequential or hierarchical "crews" that complete tasks. The mental model is a small team of specialists delivering a deliverable.
LangGraph models agents as a state graph. You define nodes (functions that mutate state), edges (transitions, conditional or static), and a reducer for state updates. The mental model is a finite-state machine with LLM-powered nodes.
AutoGen (we'll focus on v0.4+, which was a near-total rewrite from v0.2) models agents as asynchronous actors that exchange messages. Conversations between agents drive the work forward. The mental model is a group chat where each participant has different skills and tools.
All three support tool use, multi-LLM routing, and memory in some form. Where they diverge is in control flow, observability, and what "production-ready" means.
CrewAI's strength is that it gets out of your way on the 70% of use cases that are essentially a pipeline of specialist steps. Research, then summarize, then critique, then format. You don't fight the framework to express that. The Crew, Agent, and Task abstract

zh.title: CrewAI vs LangGraph vs AutoGen：哪个框架适合生产环境中的 AI 代理？
zh.summary: 基于 18 个月的生产部署经验，本文比较了三个 AI 代理框架——CrewAI（团队隐喻）、LangGraph（状态图）和 AutoGen（消息传递参与者）——解释了它们的心智模型和超越简单基准的实际权衡。框架的选择取决于用例结构、团队专业知识和运营成熟度。

title: Canvas Apps vs Code Apps in Power Apps: When low-code hits its ceiling
summary: Power Apps Code Apps are full React/TypeScript applications run locally against Dataverse, distinct from Canvas Apps and PCF controls, offering features like custom charts, unit testing, and pixel-perfect components that Canvas Apps cannot achieve. Code Apps inherit enterprise governance while providing full developer control over architecture and npm package availability.
content:
You're three months into a Power Platform engagement. The Canvas App you built is impressive — multiple screens, solid Dataverse model, Power Automate flows running clean. Stakeholders are happy. The maker on the team has already started adding screens themselves.
Then the requirements change. A filterable grid against 50,000 Dataverse rows. A custom chart with real-time updates. Pixel-perfect components from a Figma file. And then someone asks: "Can we write unit tests for the business logic?"
You're staring at Power Fx, knowing it can't get you there. This is the ceiling.
📄 This post summarizes Power Apps: Canvas Apps vs. Code Apps – When Low-Code Hits Its Ceiling originally published on AIDevMe.
When I say "Power Apps Code Apps," most people think "PCF controls." That's not it.
PCF (Power Apps Component Framework) controls are individual UI components you embed in Canvas or Model-Driven Apps. They're React components with a specific lifecycle, wrapped to integrate into the Power Apps runtime.
Code Apps are full applications. React + TypeScript projects developed in VS Code, run locally against a live Dataverse environment, deployed as solution components. They have their own routing, state management, component tree. They access Dataverse and the full Power Platform connector catalog via the Power Apps client library (@microsoft/power-apps) from JavaScript.
The scaffold comes from the official template:
npx degit github:microsoft/PowerAppsCodeApps/templates/vite my-app
cd my-app
pac auth create
pac env select --environment <Your environment ID>
npm install
pac code init --displayname "My Code App"
npm run dev

You write real TypeScript with hooks, context, custom components. Any npm package that runs in a browser is available. Full developer experience: IntelliSense, debugging, source maps, Jest test suite in CI/CD.
The app inherits enterprise governance — Microsoft Entra auth, DLP enforcement, Conditional Access — but you control the architecture. It's closer t

zh.title: Power Apps 中的 Canvas 应用与代码应用：低代码的天花板
zh.summary: Power Apps 代码应用是运行在 Dataverse 上的完整 React/TypeScript 应用程序，不同于 Canvas Apps 和 PCF 控件，提供自定义图表、单元测试和像素完美组件等 Canvas Apps 无法实现的功能。代码应用继承企业治理，同时为开发人员提供对体系结构和 npm 包可用性的完全控制。

title: How to Build a DIY USB Keylogger with ESP32-S3 (DuckLogger Tutorial)
summary: DuckLogger is an ESP32-S3-based USB keylogger written in MicroPython that captures keystrokes locally and provides Wi-Fi remote access through a web command-and-control interface, supporting features like DuckyScript injection and live keyboard input. The build uses off-the-shelf components under $10 and includes both hotspot and network station modes.
content:
Have you ever wondered how easy it is to build your own DIY USB keylogger from scratch? Using widely available and affordable hardware (under $10), you can create a powerful, Wi-Fi-enabled keystroke logger.
Enter DuckLogger, an ESP32-S3–based USB keylogger written in MicroPython. It not only logs keystrokes directly to its internal flash storage but also provides a built-in Wi-Fi access point and a sleek Web Command & Control center. 

⚠️ WARNING: For Educational Purposes Only. 
In this guide, we'll walk you through exactly how to build and configure your own DIY USB keylogger using DuckLogger.
Creating a custom PCB isn't necessary for this build. Using cheap, off-the-shelf components, DuckLogger brings a ton of features:
Keystroke Logging: Stealthily records keystrokes to a local log file.
Dual Wi-Fi Modes: Acts as its own Hotspot (Access Point mode) or connects to an existing network (Station mode).
Remote DuckyScript Injection: Inject and execute standard DuckyScript payloads remotely.
Web Command & Control Center: Easily manage logs, inject payloads, and control device settings wirelessly.
Remote Live Keyboard: Attach a virtual keyboard and send real-time keystrokes via WebSockets with near-zero latency.
Here is a look at the DuckLogger Web Command and Control Center:

(You can easily download logs, use the live keyboard, and update network settings from the web UI)
To build this ESP32-S3 keylogger, you will need:
ESP32-S3 SuperMini (The brain and Wi-Fi interface)
CH9350 HID Module (Converts USB keyboard inputs into serial data)
4 Female-to-Female Jumper Wires
A USB-C cable for flashing and power.
The wiring is incredibly straightforward. We need to connect power, ground, and UART lines between the ESP32-S3 and the CH9350.

Pin Connections:



ESP32-S3
CH9350




5V
5V


GND
GND


GP1
TX


GP2
RX



The CH9350 module has multiple operating modes controlled by onboard DIP switches.

To enable USB Host Mode (which reads keyboard inputs and outputs serial data):
Se

zh.title: 如何使用 ESP32-S3 构建 DIY USB 键盘记录器（DuckLogger 教程）
zh.summary: DuckLogger 是基于 ESP32-S3 的 USB 键盘记录器，用 MicroPython 编写，可在本地捕获击键并通过 Web 命令与控制界面提供 Wi-Fi 远程访问，支持 DuckyScript 注入和实时键盘输入等功能。该构建使用成本低于 $10 的现成组件，包括热点和网络站点模式。

title: Como importei 55 milhões de empresas para PostgreSQL em menos de 3 horas
summary: Developer optimized bulk import of 55 million Brazilian company records into PostgreSQL, reducing import time from 12+ hours to under 3 hours using batch processing and direct SQL instead of ORM overhead. Techniques including psycopg2 batching (5,000 rows per batch) and connection pooling proved significantly faster than SQLAlchemy's row-by-row approach. Demonstrates practical optimization strategies for large-scale data migrations in real-world projects.
content:
Quando decidi construir o CNPJ Aberto, que é uma plataforma gratuita de consulta de empresas brasileiras, o primeiro desafio foi óbvio: como colocar 55 milhões de registros dentro do PostgreSQL de forma rápida e repetível (a base atualiza todo mês)?
Os dados vêm dos Dados Abertos da Receita Federal, distribuídos em dezenas de ZIPs com CSVs em latin-1, separados por ;, com campos inconsistentes (muito inconsistentes!!).
A ideia deste post rápido é mostrar as técnicas que transformaram uma importação de 12+ horas em menos de 3 horas.



Tabela
Registros aproximados
Peso CSV




empresas
~55M
~4 GB


estabelecimentos
~70M
~15 GB


socios
~25M
~3 GB


simples
~35M
~2 GB


Tabelas auxiliares
~15K total
<1 MB



Total: ~25 GB de CSVs descompactados, distribuídos em ~40 arquivos ZIP. Nestes ponto é visível que os maiores problemas seriam empresas e estabelecimentos, não só para querys como para joins, que são muitos para criar um sistema legal e atrativo.
A abordagem ingênua com SQLAlchemy:
for row in csv_reader:
    empresa = Empresa(**parse_row(row))
    session.add(empresa)
    if i % 1000 == 0:
        session.commit()

Resultado: ~1.200 inserts/segundo. Para 55M de registros, isso dá ~12 horas só para a tabela empresas. Inaceitável. Eu acabei ingerindo tudo pois imaginava que > 12 horas de espera seirma melhores que > X horas de implementação e pesquisa para melhoria de queries. Talvez fossem, mas pelo desafio, fui além.
O ORM adiciona overhead em cada objeto: validação de tipo, tracking de estado, construção de SQL dinâmico.
Para melhorias, a IA acaba ajudando um pouco, não muito, ela alucinada bastante na ajuda e as vezes acaba piorando querys e criando index sem sentido, usei o Opus 4.6. Muito útil, mas o double check é necessário.
executemany com batches (4+ horas)


Removendo o ORM e usando psycopg2 direto:
BATCH_SIZE = 5000
batch = []
for row in csv_reader:
    batch.append(parse_row(row))
    if len(batch) >= BATCH_SIZE:
        cursor.executemany(INSERT_SQL, b

zh.title: 我如何在3小时内将5500万家企业导入PostgreSQL
zh.summary: 开发者通过批量处理和直接SQL而非ORM开销，将5500万条巴西公司记录的批量导入时间从12小时以上优化到3小时以内。包括psycopg2批处理和连接池在内的技术被证明比SQLAlchemy逐行方法快得多。展示了大规模数据迁移的实用优化策略。

title: OpenClaw and the Boundary Problem
summary: OpenClaw is a self-hosted personal AI system that prioritizes privacy through local-first behavior, explicit consent for pairing, and protection against data exfiltration. Unlike commercial AI assistants, it makes security boundaries visible and exposes inspectable local state through plain-file memory storage. The design emphasizes transparent documentation of risks—including the fact that agents can execute shell commands—over false claims of complete safety.
content:
This is a submission for the OpenClaw Writing Challenge.
Personal AI is usually sold as convenience.
That is the wrong frame.
If you want privacy-first, offline health tech to exist without surveillance funding it: sponsor the build → https://paintracker.ca/sponsor
The real question is not how much the assistant can do. It is what
That is why OpenClaw is interesting.
Not because it is another chatbot with better branding. Because it makes the boundary visible.
The docs describe a self-hosted gateway you run on your own machine or
OpenClaw also says the quiet part out loud.
Its security model is a personal-assistant model, not a hostile
That is the boundary problem.
A personal AI system owes the person using it a few things.
It owes them local-first behavior when possible. OpenClaw runs on your
It owes them explicit consent. OpenClaw pairing is an owner approval
It owes them restraint.
This is where most products start lying.
The FTC's Alexa case was about keeping voice and geolocation data for
It also owes them protection against exfiltration and tool abuse.
OpenClaw's own trust docs are useful here because they are not
That last part is where people get confused.
They think the fix is a better prompt.
It is not.
OpenClaw's own security docs say access control before intelligence.
And this is where release discipline stops being a side concern.
If a personal AI product claims local-first privacy, visible state,
The exact OpenClaw version was unspecified, so the argument here uses
That claim is backed by concrete architecture.
OpenClaw supports built-in channels such as Discord, Signal, Slack,
It also exposes unusually inspectable local state.
Memory is stored as plain files in the workspace, including
MEMORY.md, daily note files, and optionally DREAMS.md, and the
Just as important, the docs do not pretend this somehow dissolves risk.
OpenClaw's trust site is explicit that agents can execute shell
That honesty matters.
It means the right essay is not OpenClaw is priv

zh.title: OpenClaw和边界问题
zh.summary: OpenClaw是一个自托管的个人AI系统，通过本地优先行为、配对时的明确同意和防止数据泄露来优先考虑隐私。与商业AI助手不同，它使安全边界可见，并通过普通文件内存存储公开可检查的本地状态。该设计强调对风险的透明文档化而不是对完全安全的虚假声称。

title: AI University: 3-File Pattern to Add Any AI Provider + Offline Fallback Content
summary: Article describes a 3-file pattern for adding any new AI provider to an educational platform covering 20+ providers, using database migrations, configuration maps, and offline fallback content. Demonstrated with Reka AI integration, the approach maintains idempotence through SQL conflict resolution and graceful degradation when network unavailability occurs. Enables teams to scale multi-provider AI education with minimal boilerplate across Supabase, Dart, and backup content.
content:
AI University: 3-File Pattern to Add Any AI Provider + Offline Fallback Content



  
  
  What Is AI University


自分株式会社 includes "AI University" — a tab-based learning UI for 20+ AI providers (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, Meta, xAI, Mistral, DeepSeek, etc.):
Overview / Models / API / News tabs per provider
Quiz for comprehension check
Learning scores stored in Supabase + leaderboard
Share card generation (RenderRepaintBoundary → PNG)
Consecutive learning streaks
This post covers the 3-file pattern to add any new provider in under 15 minutes.
Adding a new AI provider requires exactly 3 changes:
-- supabase/migrations/20260412000001_seed_reka_ai_university.sql
INSERT INTO ai_university_content (provider, category, title, content, published_at)
VALUES
  ('reka', 'overview',
   'Reka AI — Multimodal-First LLM',
   '## What is Reka AI\n\nReka AI is a 2023 AI startup specializing in multimodal models...',
   '2026-04-12'),
  ('reka', 'models',
   'Reka Core / Flash / Edge Model Family',
   '## Model Overview\n\n### Reka Core\n- Largest model in the family...',
   '2026-04-12')
ON CONFLICT (provider, category) DO UPDATE
  SET title = EXCLUDED.title,
      content = EXCLUDED.content,
      published_at = EXCLUDED.published_at;

ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE makes the migration idempotent — safe to re-run.
_providerMeta Map





// lib/pages/gemini_university_v2_page.dart
static const Map<String, _ProviderMeta> _providerMeta = {
  // ... existing entries
  'reka': _ProviderMeta(
    displayName: 'Reka AI',
    emoji: '🦁',
    color: Color(0xFF7B2FF7),
  ),
};

This controls how the provider tab renders: name, emoji, accent color. If omitted, the tab still appears (driven by DB), just with default styling.
In case the Edge Function is unavailable (network error, EF not deployed yet):
static const Map<String, String> _fallback = {
  // ... existing entries
  'reka': '''## Reka AI

Multimodal-first AI startup focused on Reka Core, Flash, and Edge models.

**Key strengths**: Image

zh.title: AI大学：3文件模式用于添加任何AI提供商和离线回退内容
zh.summary: 文章描述了为教育平台添加任何新AI提供商的3文件模式，该平台覆盖20多个提供商，使用数据库迁移、配置映射和离线回退内容。通过Reka AI集成演示，该方法通过SQL冲突解决和网络不可用时的优雅降级来维持幂等性。使团队能够以最少样板跨Supabase、Dart和备份内容扩展多提供商AI教育。

title: SM-2 Spaced Repetition in Deno + Flashcard UI in Flutter Web: Building a Duolingo Competitor
summary: Developer implemented SM-2 spaced repetition algorithm server-side in Deno for language learning competing with Duolingo, paired with Flutter Web flashcard UI featuring streak tracking and statistics. The algorithm manages scheduling intervals (6 days for first correct answer, compounding multiplier on repeats) and ease factors. UI includes deck management, progress indicators, and accuracy tracking across vocabulary reviews.

zh.title: SM-2间隔重复算法在Deno中的实现 + Flutter Web闪卡UI：打造Duolingo竞品
zh.summary: 开发者在Deno中服务端实现了SM-2间隔重复算法用于语言学习市场竞争，配合Flutter Web闪卡UI，具有连胜追踪和统计功能。该算法管理调度间隔（首次正确答题6天，重复时倍增乘数）和易度因子。UI包含卡组管理、进度指示和词汇复习的准确性追踪。

title: Building a Public Guitar Gallery With 4 Users: Action Extension Pattern + Viral Design
summary: At 4 registered users, the team built a public guitar gallery as a discoverable sharing destination using an action extension pattern to avoid exceeding Supabase's 94-function deployment limit. This counter-intuitive approach demonstrates that community features should exist before users develop motivation to share. Implementation includes pagination, sorting validation, and hard-cap enforcement to manage limited deployment slots.

zh.title: 以4个用户构建公开吉他库：Action Extension模式 + 病毒式设计
zh.summary: 拥有4个注册用户的团队使用action extension模式构建了公开吉他库作为可发现的共享目标，以避免超过Supabase的94函数部署限制。这种反直觉的方法表明社区功能应在用户产生分享动力之前就存在。实现包括分页、排序验证和硬上限执行以管理有限的部署插槽。

title: Travel Planner + Whiteboard + Recipe Manager in Flutter Web: RadioListTile Migration & const Propagation
summary: Three features (travel itinerary planner, virtual whiteboard, recipe manager) were built in one session using Flutter Web UI consuming pre-existing Supabase Edge Functions, demonstrating independent frontend and backend delivery timelines. Migration from deprecated RadioListTile widgets to ListTile + Icon pattern shows Flutter 3.32+ adoption. All features use TabController for organized multi-tab productivity interfaces.

zh.title: Flutter Web中的旅行计划器 + 白板 + 食谱管理器：RadioListTile迁移与const传播
zh.summary: 三个功能（旅行行程规划器、虚拟白板、食谱管理器）在一个会话中使用Flutter Web UI构建，这些UI使用了预先存在的Supabase Edge Functions，展示了独立的前端和后端交付时间线。从已废弃的RadioListTile小部件迁移到ListTile + Icon模式展示了Flutter 3.32+的采用。所有功能都使用TabController实现组织有序的多标签生产力界面。

title: Notion-Style Team Workspace in Flutter Web: Supabase RLS EXISTS Subquery + Invite Code Pattern
summary: Team collaboration layer uses Supabase RLS with EXISTS subquery policies to enforce per-team isolation without expensive JOINs, and implements 8-character alphanumeric invite codes for email-free team joining. Architecture supports shared notes and role-based memberships (owner/member) with cascading deletes maintaining referential integrity. Efficient EXISTS-based RLS prevents users from seeing teams they don't belong to.
content:
Notion-Style Team Workspace in Flutter Web: Supabase RLS EXISTS Subquery + Invite Code Pattern



  
  
  What We Built


A team collaboration layer for 自分株式会社:
Team creation with invite codes (8-char alphanumeric)
Join via invite code (no email required)
Shared notes within a team
Supabase RLS enforces per-team isolation
-- Teams
CREATE TABLE teams (
  id          uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
  owner_id    uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  name        text NOT NULL,
  description text,
  invite_code text NOT NULL UNIQUE DEFAULT upper(substring(gen_random_uuid()::text, 1, 8)),
  created_at  timestamptz DEFAULT now()
);

-- Memberships
CREATE TABLE team_memberships (
  id         uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
  team_id    uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES teams(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id    uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  role       text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('owner', 'member')),
  joined_at  timestamptz DEFAULT now(),
  UNIQUE (team_id, user_id)
);

-- Shared Notes
CREATE TABLE team_shared_notes (
  id          uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
  team_id     uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES teams(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  shared_by   uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  note_id     uuid NOT NULL,
  shared_at   timestamptz DEFAULT now()
);

Teams are visible to owners AND members — the key is EXISTS on the membership table:
-- Users see teams they own OR are a member of
CREATE POLICY "teams_select" ON teams FOR SELECT
  USING (
    owner_id = auth.uid()
    OR EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM team_memberships tm
      WHERE tm.team_id = teams.id
        AND tm.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  );

No JOIN in the policy — EXISTS is more efficient (short-circuits on first match).
Shared notes have a three-way policy (sharer, member, or team owner):
CREATE POLICY "team_shared_notes_select" ON team_shared_notes FOR SELECT
  USING (
    shared_by = auth.uid()
    OR EXISTS

zh.title: Notion风格的Flutter Web团队工作空间：Supabase RLS EXISTS子查询 + 邀请码模式
zh.summary: 团队协作层使用Supabase RLS和EXISTS子查询策略强制执行每个团队的隔离，无需昂贵的JOIN操作，并实现了8个字符的字母数字邀请码，支持无需电子邮件的团队加入。架构支持共享笔记和基于角色的成员身份（所有者/成员），具有级联删除以维护参考完整性。高效的基于EXISTS的RLS防止用户看到他们不属于的团队。

title: CVR Tracking by Competitor Comparison Page: StatelessWidget StatefulWidget + unawaited() Pattern
summary: To measure which competitor comparison pages drive conversions, the team implemented two-signal tracking (page touch and signup attribution) that credits last-visited comparison page when users sign up. Migration from StatelessWidget to StatefulWidget with initState hooks demonstrates lifecycle dependency for analytics instrumentation. Approach enables per-page conversion ranking: "Notion comparison visitors convert at 8%, Slack at 3%."

zh.title: 按竞争对手比较页面的CVR追踪：StatelessWidget StatefulWidget + unawaited()模式
zh.summary: 为了衡量哪些竞争对手比较页面驱动转化，团队实现了两个信号追踪系统（页面访问和注册归属），在用户注册时将转化归功于最后访问的比较页面。从StatelessWidget迁移到StatefulWidget并使用initState钩子演示了分析工具的生命周期依赖。该方法支持按页面的转化排名：Notion比较访问者转化率8%，Slack为3%。

title: CRM Pipeline + Horse Racing AI in Flutter Web: Lead Scoring Formula & Kanban with LinearProgressIndicator
summary: Two competitive features were built: a CRM pipeline with server-side lead scoring (0-100 scale based on email/company/phone/referral signals) and horse-racing predictor using weighted formula (recent form, jockey/trainer win rates, track affinity). Flutter UI uses kanban columns for pipeline stages and LinearProgressIndicator for visualization. Both evaluations run server-side in Deno.

zh.title: CRM管道 + Flutter Web中的赛马AI：潜在客户评分公式 & 带LinearProgressIndicator的看板
zh.summary: 构建了两个竞争性功能：一个CRM管道，具有基于邮件/公司/电话/推荐信号的服务器端潜在客户评分（0-100分），和一个使用加权公式（最近表现、骑手/训练师胜率、赛道亲和力）的赛马预测器。Flutter UI使用看板列表示管道阶段并使用LinearProgressIndicator进行可视化。两个评估都在Deno中服务器端运行。

title: Guitar Recording Auto-Post to X: OAuth 1.0a Server-Side + Twitter Intent Fallback
summary: Guitar recordings auto-post to X through three-hop OAuth 1.0a architecture keeping credentials server-side: Flutter triggers Edge Function which calls dedicated OAuth-signed endpoint to post to X API. Implementation includes graceful fallback to Twitter intent URL when OAuth fails (timeouts, rate limits, expired keys), ensuring users can still share even if automated posting breaks. No OAuth keys exposed to client.

zh.title: 吉他录音自动发布至X：服务器端OAuth 1.0a与Twitter意图备选方案
zh.summary: 吉他录音通过三层OAuth 1.0a架构自动发布到X，凭证保留在服务器端：Flutter触发Edge Function，调用专用OAuth签名端点发布到X API。实现包括当OAuth失败时（超时、速率限制、密钥过期）优雅回退到Twitter意图URL，确保用户即使在自动发布失败时仍可分享。OAuth密钥不暴露给客户端。

title: I built an open-source Python tool to detect drift in embedding spaces
summary: drift-lens-monitor is an open-source Python package detecting statistical shifts in embedding spaces before downstream metrics degrade, supporting three drift detection approaches (statistical distance, kernel-based permutation testing, and topological shape changes). Tool addresses gap where monitoring business metrics alone misses gradual embedding drift caused by model updates, data source changes, or new user behavior. Available on PyPI.
content:
I built an open-source Python tool to detect drift in embedding spaces


Most monitoring pipelines wait for a downstream metric to break: accuracy drops, retrieval quality slips, or user-facing behavior gets worse.
By then, the shift has already happened.
I wanted a simpler way to catch changes earlier by looking directly at the embeddings themselves.
So I built drift-lens-monitor — an open-source Python package for detecting drift in embedding spaces.
GitHub: https://github.com/PRAFULREDDYM/Drift_lense

PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/drift-lens-monitor/




  
  
  What problem this solves


A lot of modern ML systems depend on embeddings:
semantic search
RAG pipelines
recommenders
clustering
classification pipelines with embedding-based features
Even when the raw system looks “healthy,” the embedding space can start changing underneath you.
That change may come from:
new user behavior
model updates
data source changes
upstream preprocessing differences
gradual distribution shift over time
If you only monitor business metrics or model accuracy, you often detect the issue late.
The idea behind this project is simple:
take snapshots of embeddings over time and compare them directly.
The package currently supports three drift detection approaches.
This is inspired by FID-style comparison, but applied to arbitrary embeddings.
At a high level, it compares the mean and covariance of two embedding distributions.
Useful when you want a compact statistical distance between a reference snapshot and a current snapshot.
This is a kernel-based, non-parametric method for comparing two samples.
I included permutation-based p-values so it can be used not just as a raw distance, but also as a statistical test.
Useful when you want a more flexible distribution comparison without assuming Gaussian structure.
This is the unusual one.
Instead of only asking whether two embedding clouds differ statistically, this looks at whether their shape changes.
It builds topological summaries over

zh.title: 我开发了一个开源Python工具来检测嵌入空间的漂移
zh.summary: drift-lens-monitor是一个开源Python包，可在下游指标恶化前检测嵌入空间的统计偏移，支持三种漂移检测方法（统计距离、基于核的排列检验和拓扑形状变化）。该工具弥补了仅监控业务指标无法捕捉由模型更新、数据源变化或新用户行为引起的逐步嵌入漂移的空白。可在PyPI上获取。

title: ArchRad 0.3.0 — Two commands to find architecture violations in your existing stack
summary: ArchRad 0.3.0 addresses cold-start architecture validation by adding archrad init command that generates IR graphs from existing Docker Compose files in under 60 seconds, eliminating manual JSON schema authoring. Tool automatically infers node types from Docker image patterns and creates edges from depends_on directives and connection URL environment variables. Two commands enable violation detection without prior schema learning.
content:
All our early release of ArchRad has assumed one thing: that you already have an IR graph of your system to validate. You needed to author JSON by hand, learn the schema, map your services yourself before you could see a single violation.
The cold start problem
The feedback we kept hearing — from developers who found ArchRad on npm, from platform engineers who tried it in CI — was always the same: the first step is too hard. Before you could run archrad validate, you had to write an IR graph. Before you could write an IR graph, you had to understand the schema. Most people never got that far.
That's the cold start problem. And archrad init is the fix.
archrad init generates a valid IR graph from artifacts you already have. Starting with Docker Compose.
archrad init --from docker-compose.yml --output graph.json
archrad validate --ir graph.json

Two commands. No JSON authoring. No schema learning. If you have a docker-compose.yml, you can see your architecture violations in under 60 seconds.
Every service in your Compose file becomes a node in the IR graph. The node type is inferred from the image:



Image pattern
node.type




postgres, mysql, mariadb, timescale, cockroach
postgres


redis, memcached
cache


rabbitmq, kafka, nats, pulsar
queue


nginx, traefik, caddy, kong, envoy
gateway


elasticsearch, opensearch
search


minio, localstack
storage


unknown image + published ports

gateway (HTTP-facing fallback)


unknown image, no ports

service (with warning)



depends_on → edges
Both list and object forms are supported. Each dependency becomes a directed edge in the IR graph.
Connection URL detection
This is where it gets interesting. If a service has a DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, AMQP_URL, MONGODB_URI, or any of eight other connection URL environment variables, and the hostname in that URL matches another service in the compose file — archrad init creates an edge.
This is how it catches direct database access automatically. You didn't have to tell it. It found i

zh.title: ArchRad 0.3.0——两条命令查找现有堆栈中的架构违规
zh.summary: ArchRad 0.3.0通过添加archrad init命令解决冷启动架构验证问题，该命令可在60秒内从现有Docker Compose文件生成IR图，无需手动编写JSON模式。该工具可从Docker镜像模式自动推断节点类型，从depends_on指令和连接URL环境变量创建边。两条命令无需事先学习模式即可启用违规检测。
